caveats: a: No six-digit occupational distribution was found.
a: The frozen compensation ratio combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts and may be affected by imports and outsourced production embedded in firm revenue.
rho: Representative implementation evidence for LMM plumbing-products manufacturers was not found.
rho: Digital readiness varies between branded catalog businesses and contract metalworking plants.
e: The assigned 13-firm count is margin-bridged rather than an observed EBITDA-band census.
e: Brands may be classified as manufacturers despite outsourcing substantial production.
s5: No owner-age or succession dataset specific to plumbing fitting manufacturers was found.
s5: Brand, product-line, or tooling acquisitions without company control must be excluded.
q: No representative contract dataset was available.
q: Retention differs between premium branded trim, specified commercial products, and commodity private-label fittings.
d5: Housing starts and WaterSense adoption are demand drivers, not direct measures of NAICS 332913 output.
d5: A faucet aerator retrofit can improve efficiency without replacement of the full fitting.
o: This measures operator requirement for remaining product demand, not domestic share or customer retention.
o: Import substitution changes geography of the operator and can still erode the screened U.S. basket.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

identity.title: Plumbing Fixture Fitting and Trim Manufacturing

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat product and replenishment sales through wholesale distribution, building-product channels, OEM and private-label programs, and project specifications, priced per unit or lot.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive plants, plumbing contractors, distributors and importers without manufacturing, fixture installation services, shell brands, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of faucets, valves, shower fittings, drains, and other plumbing fixture trim supplied repeatedly to external distributors, builders, OEMs, retailers, and institutional customers.

a.bridge: BLS reports that fabricated-metal manufacturing has 1.439 million workers and substantial production employment, including 14.09% in other production occupations, alongside office clerks and engineering technicians. I map planning, forecasting, order entry, customer service, product-document, procurement, marketing, and routine engineering-support tasks to AI exposure while excluding forming, machining, casting, plating, assembly, testing, packing, maintenance, and accountable product approval. The three-digit mix may overstate heavy fabrication and understate branded commercial work in plumbing trim.
caveats: No six-digit occupational distribution was found.
The frozen compensation ratio combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts and may be affected by imports and outsourced production embedded in firm revenue.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted task study from 15-20 independent plumbing-fitting manufacturers, split across production, engineering, quality, warehouse, sales, customer service, marketing, and administration, would materially change the range.
a.rationale: AI can assist catalog and specification search, quote and order handling, demand planning, procurement, customer support, product-content generation, warranty triage, and quality-document analysis. Most factory work and physical quality control remains embodied.

d5.bridge: Census reports June 2026 housing starts at a 1.427 million annual rate, 3.5% above June 2025, while permits were 2.3% below the prior year. EPA reports more than 45,900 WaterSense-labeled product models and strong replacement savings from efficient faucets. I translate mixed construction activity plus continuing efficiency-driven replacement into broadly stable to modestly growing real demand; this may overstate domestic manufacturing because imports can capture unit growth and understate repair, remodeling, and commercial demand not tied to starts.
caveats: Housing starts and WaterSense adoption are demand drivers, not direct measures of NAICS 332913 output.
A faucet aerator retrofit can improve efficiency without replacement of the full fitting.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year constant-price shipment forecasts by residential new-build, repair and remodel, commercial, and institutional channel, reconciled to domestic production and imports, would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: New construction, remodeling, replacement of leaking or dated products, and water-efficiency upgrades sustain demand. Housing cyclicality, higher rates, import competition, durable product lives, and simple aerator retrofits limit manufacturer volume.

caveats: The assigned 13-firm count is margin-bridged rather than an observed EBITDA-band census.
Brands may be classified as manufacturers despite outsourcing substantial production.
e.change_evidence: A firm census linking the SUSB-band population to domestic manufacturing content, ownership, recurring external sales, channel mix, and normalized EBITDA would materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: Operating manufacturers commonly serve recurring distributors, builders, retailers, and institutional channels, fitting the external repeat-customer lens. Captive production, import-only brands, project-only custom shops, and firms outside the EBITDA band reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 332913 ($833,589,848) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 332913 ($6,546,937,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $697,670 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 332913); class <5: 52 firms, avg revenue/firm $367,154 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $72,036 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,927,882 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $378,251 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 6 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,398,167 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $666,720 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $14,535,308 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,851,827 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 11 firms, avg revenue/firm $63,771,455 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,511,959 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 12 firms, avg revenue/firm $465,353,083 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $91,302,275 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 13

caveats: This measures operator requirement for remaining product demand, not domestic share or customer retention.
Import substitution changes geography of the operator and can still erode the screened U.S. basket.
o.change_evidence: Builder, distributor, and OEM roadmaps quantifying fixture-free design, modular retrofits, additive production, customer insourcing, and replacement of mechanical trim by alternative systems would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Remaining demand for faucets, valves, drains, and trim requires accountable physical manufacture, finishing, assembly, testing, and certification. Smart controls and digital sales do not replace water-handling hardware, though simplified retrofits, imports, and customer outsourcing can shift who manufactures it.

caveats: No representative contract dataset was available.
Retention differs between premium branded trim, specified commercial products, and commodity private-label fittings.
q.change_evidence: Distributor, retailer, OEM, and private-label agreements showing rebates, price resets, cost-downs, promotions, chargebacks, and renewal outcomes would materially change retention.
q.rationale: Catalog and branded pricing can preserve savings between resets, especially for differentiated style or specified products. Wholesale concentration, retailer programs, private-label bids, commodity substitutes, rebates, and annual price negotiations pass a meaningful share to customers over five years.

caveats: Representative implementation evidence for LMM plumbing-products manufacturers was not found.
Digital readiness varies between branded catalog businesses and contract metalworking plants.
rho.change_evidence: Before-and-after labor hours, error rates, quote and order cycle times, forecast accuracy, warranty closure, and content throughput from building-product manufacturers using AI would materially move the estimate.
rho.rationale: Standard ERP, CRM, catalog, e-commerce, and product-information workflows should permit supervised AI implementation within five years. Legacy product data, distributor integrations, aesthetic judgment, customer-specific specifications, and the need for human engineering and quality approval constrain full realization.

caveats: No owner-age or succession dataset specific to plumbing fitting manufacturers was found.
Brand, product-line, or tooling acquisitions without company control must be excluded.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, Capital IQ, brand and plant ownership changes, and building-products broker interviews reconciled to the 13-firm estimate would materially change the range.
s5.rationale: Brands, channel relationships, specifications, tooling, and product certifications can make firms transferable to strategic building-products groups. With only 13 estimated target firms and no observed industry transfer denominator, realized availability is highly discrete and uncertain.

principal_driver: Recurring replacement and construction demand for physically indispensable water-control hardware.
principal_weakness: A very small estimated target universe and low labor intensity limit scalable AI-driven value creation.
